statement: NR4A3 rearrangement is near-pathognomonic for EMC (~90-98% of cases); NR4A3 is the invariant 3' partner regardless of the 5' gene.
notes: Basis for treating the fusion, not point mutations, as the therapeutic anchor.

statement: EWSR1::NR4A3 is the dominant fusion (~62-79%, ~75% typical); TAF15::NR4A3 ~16-27%; TCF12/FUS/TFG make up most of the rest.
notes: Subtype prevalence; TAF15 is a minority so TAF15-specific evidence is inherently scarcer (single modern model USZ22-EMC2).

statement: TAF15::NR4A3 (and other non-EWSR1 variant fusions) correlate with rhabdoid phenotype and high-grade morphology.
notes: Reason not to pool subtypes; TAF15 may have a distinct transcriptional/response program.

statement: The fusion is a transcriptionally active oncoprotein: EWSR1/NR4A3 binds a response element in the PPARG promoter and transactivates it (gain-of-function).
notes: Gain-of-function, NOT addiction proof. Heterologous reporter caveat.

statement: EMC has a genomically quiet, clonal landscape: the fusion is the shared founding lesion across primary + metastases; no recurrent secondary clinically-actionable driver in metastatic disease.
locator: Trio WGS: SV 53->46->163; Davis 2017: no recurrent driver
notes: Supports fusion/lineage-directed strategy over mutation-by-mutation precision oncology. Late mets can acquire new SVs.

statement: RET expression is elevated in metastatic EMC and folate-receptor expression occurs in a minority.
notes: RNA elevation is a MARKER of the fusion state, NOT proof of RET dependency (must be perturbationally tested). Do not treat as RET addiction.

statement: In DepMap (24Q4, n=1178 CRISPR lines), NR4A3 is non-essential in dividing cells (mean gene effect +0.023, 0/1178 lines dependent); NR4A1 -0.115 (0.5%), NR4A2 -0.05 (0.3%).
notes: SURROGATE: no EMC line in DepMap. Supports a proliferative-compartment therapeutic window; does NOT test EMC fusion dependency (wild-type NR4A3 != fusion).

statement: FET-fusion sarcomas are addicted to their fusion as a class: FLI1 in Ewing shows gene effect -0.93, 74% of lines dependent (n=27).
notes: ANALOGY only. Raises the prior that EMC depends on its fusion; the shared EWS low-complexity domain drives Ewing addiction, so it supports 'EMC is probably fusion-addicted', NOT 'the NR4A3 effector is essential'.

statement: No direct loss-of-function experiment (RNAi/CRISPR/ASO knockdown of NR4A3 or the fusion with a growth/survival readout) in any human EMC cell line exists in the literature.
notes: EMC fusion dependence is a strong multi-pillar PRIOR, not a demonstrated dependency. The acute-degradation (dTAG) experiment is make-or-break and needs a wet lab.

statement: NR4A3 is germline loss-of-function-constrained in humans (gnomAD pLI ~0.9999, LOEUF 0.37); NR4A2 even more so (pLI 1.0); only NR4A1 is LoF-tolerant.
notes: Developmental/tissue-specific essentiality, not proliferative. 'Dispensable in DepMap therefore safe to degrade' is NOT a valid inference. CNS/NR4A2 is the safety watch-zone.

statement: Myeloid-specific NR4A1<->NR4A3 redundancy: combined Nr4a1/Nr4a3 loss causes AML in mice while single nulls do not.
notes: Makes NR4A1-sparing MANDATORY for any NR4A degrader (avoid the NR4A1+NR4A3 combination).

statement: NR4A3 is an orphan nuclear receptor with no validated selective small-molecule ligand suitable for building a degrader; the EWSR1/TAF15 portion is low-complexity/intrinsically disordered.
notes: Why direct fusion targeting is highest-specificity but currently low-tractability. Governs the in-repo cryptic-pocket degrader program's honest framing.

statement: B7-H3 (CD276) is near-universally expressed across sarcoma lines (mean 5.73 log2TPM+1, 99% of lines) as an EMC surrogate.
notes: SURROGATE. RNA != accessible surface protein; requires EMC tissue/proteomics confirmation before any ADC/CAR program.

statement: PRAME is expressed in 53% of sarcoma lines and HIGH in myxoid-class (7.6) and synovial (7.2); MAGE-A4 (7%) and NY-ESO-1 (5%) are low.
notes: SURROGATE + cell lines silence cancer-testis antigens (CTA reads are lower bounds). Relative order PRAME >> MAGE-A4/NY-ESO-1 is the trustworthy part.

statement: PPARG over-expression in EMC is a direct transcriptional consequence of the fusion; it does not establish whether agonism, antagonism, or neither suppresses the tumor.
notes: Explicit anti-pattern guard: do NOT treat PPARG expression as PPARG addiction. Belongs in a perturbational screen.
